The cost of 40 pounds of carob at $2.55 per pound is 40 * 2.55 = $110.00.

The total cost of the candy is $54.00 + $110.00 = $164.00.

The total weight of the candy is 20 + 40 = 60 pounds.

The answer is 2.80.

The cost of 20 pounds of chocolate at $2.7 per pound is 20 * 2.7 = $54.00.

The cost of 40 pounds of carob at $2.55 per pound is 40 * 2.55 = $110.00.

The total cost of the candy is $54.00 + $110.00 = $164.00.

The total weight of the candy is 20 + 40 = 60 pounds.

The cost per pound of the mixture is the total cost divided by the total weight, which is $164.00 / 60 pounds = $2.80 per pound.

The answer is 2.80.
